normalized_title: City of Melville Local Planning Policy 1.8 - Crime Prevention Through Environmental Design of Buildings
scope_summary: Applies Crime Prevention Through Environmental Design (CPTED) principles, drawn from the WAPC 'Designing Out Crime' Planning Guidelines (2006), to all residential and non-residential development in the City of Melville. Requires passive surveillance (ground-floor windows viewing the primary/secondary street, overlooking of car parks and open space reserves), avoidance of blank walls or anti-graffiti treatment, visually permeable front fencing, and for non-residential and multiple dwelling developments, avoidance of blind corners/recessed alcoves and lighting or CCTV for parking areas. Where these elements conflict with R-Codes visual privacy provisions, the R-Codes prevail.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: fencing within the front setback of properties

cross_references.cited: WAPC Designing Out Crime - Planning Guidelines (2006)
State Planning Policy 7.3 Residential Design Codes Volume 1 (R-Codes)
Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015

quality_flags: clean extraction, 3 pages, full text legible
summary.json title ends mid-phrase ('...Environmental Design of') because the title wraps over two lines; full title is 'Crime Prevention Through Environmental Design of Buildings'
administrative review 27/8/2019 (Council Resolution 18/6/2019); print footer 'This Version: 31/10/2019'
